content.content:
1→## MVP: transflow (Minimal Viable Implementation)
     2→
     3→Purpose: deliver a basic, working transflow that can apply OpenRewrite recipes, heal build errors via LangGraph planner/reducer jobs with parallel options, and open a GitLab MR.
     4→
     5→## Implementation Status
     6→
     7→✅ **Fully Implemented:**
     8→- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
     9→- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    10→- YAML configuration parsing and validation
    11→- Git operations (clone, branch, commit)
    12→- Diff validation and application utilities
    13→
    14→⚠️ **Partially Implemented:**
    15→- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer infrastructure exists, orchestration in progress)
    16→- Self-healing config parsing and data structures
    17→- Job templates for healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
    18→
    19→❌ **Not Implemented:**
    20→- GitLab MR integration
    21→- Model registry in `ployman` CLI
    22→- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`)
    23→- KB (knowledge base) read/write for learning
    24→
    25→### In-Scope (Must Work)
    26→
    27→- OpenRewrite recipe execution
    28→  - Reuse existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline (services/openrewrite-jvm, API/CLI `ploy arf transform`).
    29→  - Input: repo URL + branch, ordered recipe IDs. Output: local patch/commit applied to a workflow branch.
    30→
    31→ - LLM plan and exec
    32→  - `llm-exec` (Stream 2/Phase 1): run model with prompts + prefetched context (repo paths, HTTPS URLs), apply generated patch, commit; execute as Nomad jobs via internal/orchestration.
    33→  - `llm-plan` (Stream 2/Phase 2): produce a list of `llm-exec` steps (sequential only in MVP) from repo state + last error.
    34→  - Model registry: minimal CRUD in `ployman` CLI with schema validation; stored under `llms` namespace.
    35→  - MCP tools: declare per-step (MCP spec), env-only config; prefetch context per execution (no cache/persist).
    36→
    37→- Build check (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    38→  - Use existing build API to verify the code builds without deploying.
    39→  - Client: `internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush` (POST tarball to controller `POST /v1/apps/:app/builds?sha=...&env=dev[&lane=...]`).
    40→  - Server handler: `internal/build/trigger.go` performs lane detection (if lane not provided), builds artifacts (Unikraft/OSv/OCI/etc.), signs, generates SBOM, uploads to storage; it does not deploy to Nomad.
    41→  - Success criteria: HTTP 200; response includes `lane`, `image` or `dockerImage`. For logs, optional `GET /v1/apps/:app/logs`.
    42→  - Status endpoint (optional): `GET /v1/apps/:app/status` for additional visibility.
    43→  - App naming: derive unique app name `tfw-<transflow-id>-<timestamp>` unless explicitly provided.
    44→  - Lane override: allow explicit `lane` in transflow; fallback to auto-detect when absent.
    45→  - Timeout: request timeout configurable per run; default 10m for build check.
    46→
    47→ - Git operations + MR (Stream 3)
    48→  - Create a workflow branch at start; commit changes from steps; push branch to origin using provided token envs.
    49→  - GitLab MR: infer project from `target_repo`, target branch is `base_ref`; envs `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN`.
    50→  - Apply default MR labels/scope: `ploy`, `tfl` (if available).
    51→
    52→- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer as jobs)
    53→  - Planner job: Input = repo metadata + last_error + KB snapshot; Output = `plan.json` with parallel options: human‑step, llm‑exec, orw‑generated.
    54→  - Orchestrator fan‑out: run each option as an independent Nomad job (branch); first success wins; cancel others.
    55→  - Reducer job: Input = branch results + winner; Output = next actions (usually stop; else new `plan.json`).
    56→  - KB: cases and summaries persisted in SeaweedFS; locks via Consul KV; error/patch dedup via normalized signatures/fingerprints.
    57→  - Consistent learning & dedup: see `roadmap/transflow/kb.md`. Planner reads `summary.json`; branches write `cases/` and patch blobs; compactor maintains summaries and optional vector bundles.
    58→  - Job types:
    59→    - human-step: wait for human MR/commit; runner polls branch; success if build passes afterward.
    60→    - llm-exec: generate diff-only patch (MCP tools allowed); apply and build-check.
    61→    - orw-gen → openrewrite: generate ORW recipe (class/coords) via LLM; run OpenRewrite job; build-check.
    62→
    63→- Transflow runner (orchestrator)
    64→  - Parse `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` and run steps sequentially: recipe → llm-plan → llm-exec → MR.
    65→  - Minimal logging; write per-step logs and final summary.
    66→
    67→### What Already Exists (Leverage)
    68→
    69→- OpenRewrite execution: `services/openrewrite-jvm`, ARF endpoints and CLI (`internal/cli/arf/*`, `api/arf/*`).
    70→- Git clone/diff/commit helpers: `api/arf/git_operations.go` (extend with push).
    71→- Lane/build/deploy system (not used in MVP flow): lane detection, Nomad templates, push deploy (`internal/cli/common/deploy.go`).
    72→- Git provider envs expected for MR (GitLab): set via environment variables.
    73→
    74→### New Work (Minimal)
    75→
    76→- New `transflow` orchestrator
    77→  - CLI entry: `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`.
    78→  - Step engine: call existing ARF recipe execution; implement LLM plan/exec runners; manage workflow branch lifecycle; run Build check before MR.
    79→
    80→- LLM runners
    81→  - `llm-exec`: container/job that receives model+prompts+context; produces unified diff or patch; apply and commit (used in parallel healing options).
    82→  - MCP injection via env; context prefetcher for repo files and HTTPS URLs.
    83→
    84→- Model registry
    85→  - `ployman models {list|get|add|update|delete}` with basic schema validation; stored alongside existing recipe metadata.
    86→
    87→ - MR integration (GitLab)
    88→  - Push branch using token env; call GitLab REST to create/update MR (project inferred from `target_repo` URL, source branch, target=`base_ref`), adding default labels `ploy`,`tfl` when supported.
    89→
    90→- Build check integration
    91→  - Implement a `build` step that tars the workspace and invokes `SharedPush` with `IsPlatform=false`, `Environment=dev`, and optional lane override from transflow.
    92→  - Expand `DeployConfig` to include `Timeout time.Duration`; `SharedPush` honors `config.Timeout` sourced from transflow's global `build_timeout`.
    93→  - Treat non-200 as build failure; record response JSON in step logs for diagnostics.
    94→
    95→ - LangGraph planner/reducer integration
    96→  - Add job templates and CLI/orchestrator glue to invoke planner after a failing build, and reducer after branch completion.
    97→  - Persist `plan.json`, branch job results, and a compact run manifest; first‑success‑wins cancellation.
    98→
    99→### Interfaces (High-Level)
   100→
   101→- Input: `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` (id, repo, branch, steps; global lane/build_timeout).
   102→- Output: GitLab MR link, `plan.json`/branch results, step logs, final summary JSON.
   103→- Env: `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN` for MR; model/MCP creds via env only.
   104→
   105→### Out of Scope (MVP)
   106→
   107→- Parallel execution and branch-per-exec merging.
   108→- Secure network policy and allowlists.
   109→- Budgets/limits (tokens, cost, time).
   110→- Static analysis, vulnerability scans, compile gates.
   111→- Deploy/test gates and lane-driven runtime checks.
   112→- SeaweedFS/Consul KV persistence beyond what ARF already uses implicitly.
   113→- Auto-formatters outside of step-specific behavior.
   114→- End-to-end integration tests; coverage thresholds.
   115→
   116→### Risks and Mitigations
   117→
   118→- LLM determinism: constrain outputs to unified diff/patch; validate before apply.
   119→- Context quality: ensure prefetch resolves globs/URLs deterministically at run time; log sources.
   120→- MR creation: handle idempotency (update existing MR for same branch).
   121→
   122→### Next Steps
   123→
   124→1) Add `ploy transflow run` CLI + YAML parser. 2) Wire recipe step to ARF. 3) Implement LangGraph planner/reducer jobs and orchestrator fan‑out logic. 4) Implement LLM‑exec runner with MCP/env/context prefetch (for branch options). 5) Implement git push + GitLab MR creation. 6) Minimal logs and summary output.
   125→### Test Case: JDK 11 → 17 Migration
   126→
   127→- Minimal transform request (single OpenRewrite recipe step):
   128→
   129→POST /v1/transforms
   130→Content-Type: application/json
   131→
   132→{
   133→  "id": "java11to17",
   134→  "target_repo": "https://git.example.com/org/app.git",
   135→  "base_ref": "refs/heads/main",
   136→  "target_branch": "refs/heads/main",
   137→  "lane": "C",
   138→  "steps": [
   139→    {
   140→      "type": "recipe",
   141→      "engine": "openrewrite",
   142→      "recipes": [
   143→        "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17"
   144→      ]
   145→    }
   146→  ]
   147→}
   148→
   149→- Expected flow:
   150→  1) Apply ORW recipe; commit on workflow/<id>/<timestamp>.
   151→  2) Build check via /v1/apps/:app/builds (no deploy).
   152→  3) If build fails, run LangGraph planner job; branches: human-step, llm-exec, orw-gen→openrewrite.
   153→  4) First success wins; reducer finalizes next actions (usually stop).
   154→
